questions.question: According to the Editorial Calendar, what is the minimum absolute frequency for posting Reels per week?

answerOptions.rationale: While a lower frequency might seem easier, the documentation explicitly sets a higher minimum to maintain growth.
The editorial calendar defines 3 Reels as the absolute minimum, with 4 being optional if possible.
The strategy prioritizes consistency over extreme volume, and 5 is not mentioned as a requirement.
Daily posting is only required for Stories, not for produced video content like Reels.

questions.hint: Consider the minimum threshold established for the primary reach format.

questions.question: Which content format is identified as the best for converting users into 'saved' posts and engaged followers?

answerOptions.rationale: Stories are used for daily interaction and behind-the-scenes content rather than high-save educational value.
YouTube is currently treated only as a secondary channel for repurposing existing content.
Reels are designated as the primary tool for reach and attracting new followers rather than deep engagement.
Carousels are noted for their ability to convert users into saves and high-engagement followers.

questions.hint: Think about the format that allows for multi-page educational deep dives.

questions.question: By what time on Tuesday must Michel send the briefing to the editor to avoid being 'poked' on WhatsApp?

answerOptions.rationale: While early is better, the specific deadline for the editor to take action is slightly later in the day.
The rules state that if the briefing isn't sent by Tuesday at noon, the editor is instructed to request it.
Late afternoon would delay the 48-hour editing SLA significantly.
Monday is for planning and briefing preparation, but the hard deadline for delivery is Tuesday.

questions.hint: This deadline occurs right in the middle of the recording day.

questions.question: Which of these metrics is considered the MOST important for evaluating educational content success?

answerOptions.rationale: The documentation dismisses likes as a 'vanity metric' that doesn't reflect real value.
Views indicate reach, but they don't necessarily prove the educational value provided to the user.
Saves are the top priority for educational content, with a target goal of staying above $3\%$.
While there is a goal for 10k followers, individual post success is measured by user intent like saving.

questions.hint: Identify the action a user takes when they want to reference the information later.

questions.question: What is the specific camera setting Michel is instructed to avoid because it makes files too large for the editor?

answerOptions.rationale: 4K is actually the required resolution for high-quality source material.
The recording process explicitly forbids 60 fps because it inflates file size unnecessarily; 30 fps is preferred.
The documents do not mention Cinematic Mode, focusing instead on framerate and resolution.
1080p would result in smaller files, but the goal is high quality (4K) at a manageable framerate.

questions.hint: Focus on the setting related to the number of frames captured every second.

questions.question: What advice did the mentor, Gabriel, give regarding the delegation of tasks?

answerOptions.text: Delegate caption writing to the editors
answerOptions.rationale: Michel currently retains responsibility for writing post descriptions and CTAs.
Gabriel emphasizes that responding to comments is a vital way to build a real community and should stay with the creator.
Michel still does all the scripting and is only considering a framework for future delegation.
Automation isn't mentioned; the focus is on the human connection in the comments section.

questions.hint: Consider which task involves the most direct personal connection with the audience.

questions.question: According to the 'Métricas e KPIs' document, how long should the team wait before judging a post as a failure?

answerOptions.rationale: Early data can be misleading as the algorithm takes time to find the right audience segments.
While closer, the specific rule in the strategy allows for one more day of performance tracking.
The rules state to wait at least 72 hours, as the Instagram algorithm can sometimes take days to distribute a post.
Waiting a full week is unnecessary for initial performance assessment, though late virality can happen after two weeks.

questions.hint: Think of a three-day window for the algorithm to stabilize.

questions.question: Which of the following is an 'autonomous' decision the editor (Carol or Jana) can make without asking Michel?

answerOptions.rationale: The CTA is a critical strategic element that must be clear in the briefing or checked before delivery.
Editors have autonomy to pick music from the approved Drive folder or Spotify playlist.

answerOptions.text: Determining if the hook is strong enough
answerOptions.rationale: The editor must specifically ask Michel if the hook (first 3 seconds) is strong enough before final delivery.
The theme is determined by Michel in the briefing and locked two weeks in advance.

questions.hint: Look for a stylistic element that is supported by a pre-approved library or playlist.

questions.question: What was identified as a major 'positioning problem' during Michel's mentorship session?

answerOptions.text: The video quality is too low
answerOptions.rationale: The mentor actually noted that the content is good, but the unique angle is what's missing.
Michel was unable to clearly define the specific transformation he delivers, leading to a generic productivity POV.

answerOptions.text: He is posting too many Carousels
answerOptions.rationale: Carousels are actually performing well; the issue is with the underlying brand message.

answerOptions.text: The use of AI tools is confusing the audience
answerOptions.rationale: As an educator of AI, this is his niche, but he lacks a unique perspective within that niche.

questions.hint: Recall the question Michel couldn't answer clearly during his talk with Gabriel.

questions.question: What is the mandatory visual style for captions (legends) in the Reels according to the technical specs?

answerOptions.text: Yellow text with no border at the top of the screen
answerOptions.rationale: The specs require specific colors and positioning to avoid UI interference and ensure readability.

answerOptions.text: White text with a thin black outline, centered-low
answerOptions.rationale: The standard is white text with a black outline, positioned in the center-bottom (but not the absolute bottom).

answerOptions.text: Multi-colored text that changes with each word
answerOptions.rationale: Consistency is prioritized over flashy changes; the template defines a specific uniform style.

answerOptions.text: Black text in a solid white box, aligned left
answerOptions.rationale: The defined template uses an outline rather than a solid background box for the text.

questions.hint: The position must avoid being cut off by the Instagram UI elements like the description or buttons.
